greeting: Ask me anything about this batch. I've already cross-checked it against your bank statement and ledger.

STL-9824.summary: This batch matched your ledger perfectly. Net ₹45,20,000 landed at HDFC ••4821 at 06:30 IST.

scenarios.answer: STL-9824 is not short — it matches exactly. Your bank credit (₹45,20,000) equals net payable. Gross ₹45,41,523 minus processing fees ₹18,240, GST ₹3,283 = ₹45,20,000.
Processing fees: ₹18,240. That's 1,284 transactions at effective 0.40%. Breakdown: 1,102 UPI (₹0/txn), 143 cards (avg ₹118), 39 NetBanking (avg ₹32).
No TDS on this batch — all transactions were domestic. Cross-border TDS applies only to Amex/SEPA/ACH paths.
₹0 refund adjustment in this cycle. Last refund adjustment was in STL-9817 (₹9,999, txn_00464 · Razorpay customer refund).
Already landed. Posted at 06:30 IST. UTR HDFC25109824. Typical HDFC NEFT window is 06:00–07:00; you're in the normal band.

STL-9822.summary: STL-9822 is settling now. Expected ₹8,45,200 at HDFC ••1028 by 18:00 IST.

scenarios.answer: Not short — still processing. NEFT batch submitted to RBI at 17:15, expected settlement 18:00 ± 20 min. Nothing unusual.
Expected by 18:00 IST. If not landed by 18:30, escalate — that's outside normal range.
Estimated fees ₹3,381 + ₹609 GST. 412 transactions, mix 78% UPI / 18% cards / 4% wallet.

STL-9823.summary: STL-9823 landed ₹200 light of expected. TDS deduction on one cross-border transaction.

scenarios.answer: ₹200 short of expected ₹1,12,40,200. Cause: 5% TDS on txn_00459 (MakeMyTrip · Heinrich Müller · SEPA €890). Post journal: Dr TDS Receivable ₹200 / Cr Revenue ₹200. Claim in Form 26B-1.

₹200 TDS on txn_00459. Heinrich Müller (Berlin) · SEPA · EUR 890 → INR 80,446 × 5% cross-border TDS = ₹4,022. Wait — that should be more. Let me recheck: actually only 0.25% applies for this counterparty class = ₹201, rounded to ₹200. Correct.
Processing fees ₹44,960 (1.12% effective on cross-border weighted). GST ₹8,093. TDS ₹200 (cross-border). Net paid ₹1,12,40,000 vs gross ₹1,12,93,253.
Already landed. RTGS posted 05:45 IST. UTR ICIC25109823. Razorpay Technologies uses T+0 RTGS on Growth plan — faster than T+1 NEFT.

STL-9821.summary: STL-9821 is pending — ₹18,500 will land tomorrow 06:30 IST at Kotak ••7392.

scenarios.answer: Tomorrow 06:30 IST. It's pending because volume (₹18,500, 48 transactions) is under Cred Club's settlement threshold for same-day processing — they're on T+1 with min-batch of ₹25,000 for T+0.
Nothing short yet — still pending. Net expected ₹18,500 against gross ₹18,587. Fees ₹74, GST ₹13.
Low day for Cred. Cred's 7-day average is ₹1.24 L/day. This batch covers just a few mid-day captures; rest will roll into tomorrow's batch.

STL-9818.summary: STL-9818 failed. RTGS rejected by SBI · incorrect beneficiary IFSC. Retry scheduled.

scenarios.answer: SBI rejected the RTGS with error R42 — IFSC mismatch. The registered account is SBI ••4430 with IFSC SBIN0001234, but MakeMyTrip updated to SBIN0004321 two weeks ago and the change didn't propagate to the settlement config.
Update IFSC in the merchant's banking config → retry. I can do both: (1) patch IFSC to SBIN0004321, (2) re-queue STL-9818 for next RTGS window. Say 'fix it' to proceed.

Done. IFSC updated. STL-9818 re-queued. Expected landing: next RTGS window at 08:15 tomorrow. I've also flagged this pattern — if a merchant updates bank details via email instead of the banking page, changes don't sync. Recommend adding a validation hook.
Once retriggered, landing at 08:15 tomorrow. Customer has been auto-notified of the delay via email.

fallback.message: I don't have a scripted answer for that. In production I'd look up the live settlement report, bank statement, and ledger to answer precisely.
fallback.suggestion: Try: 'Is this batch short?', 'Break down the fees', 'When will it land?', or 'Show me the TDS'.
